questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 45° and angle B measures 65°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So angle C = 180° − 45° − 65° = 70°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one angle measuring 35°. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.explanation: One angle is 90° (right angle), another is 35°. The third angle = 180° − 90° − 35° = 55°.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, the vertex angle (the angle between the two equal sides) measures 80°. What is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. If the vertex angle is 80°, then each base angle = (180° − 80°) ÷ 2 = 50°.

questions.question: Two angles of a triangle measure 28° and 112°. Is this triangle acute, right, or obtuse?
questions.explanation: The third angle = 180° − 28° − 112° = 40°. Since one angle (112°) is greater than 90°, the triangle is obtuse.

questions.question: In a right triangle, one of the acute angles measures 62°. What is the measure of the other acute angle?
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the two acute angles sum to 90°. So the other acute angle = 90° − 62° = 28°.

questions.question: An equilateral triangle has all sides equal. What is the measure of each angle in an equilateral triangle?
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, all three angles are equal. Since the sum is 180°, each angle = 180° ÷ 3 = 60°.
